chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 1776 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case (Priority) 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of CMA No.6799/2024 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case 16.12.2024 Mr. Shabbir Ali Bozdar, Advocate for Petitioner Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Additional Advocate General Sindh along with Shahid Ahmed Abro, Controller of Examinations, SPSC >>>>>..<<<<< At the very outset, learned AAG Sindh files comments on behalf of respondents No.2 and 3, taken on record. In rebuttal in para-6 of the petition, the answering respondents have mentioned as under;- “It is respectfully submitted that, the representation / appeal of petitioner is not traceable in this office thus is advised to file fresh under regulation 161 of RMR 2023 for redressal of his grievance. However, it is again reiterated that petitioner failed to qualify written part of CCE 2021 having secured 25 marks out of 100 in English Essay.” When confronted with above, learned counsel for the petitioner draws attention of the Court towards page-35 and submits that he had already submitted the same and since they have not traced it out, he undertakes to file a fresh Representation before the respondents within 15 (fifteen) days from today. Learned AAG assisted by the officer present in Court undertakes that in case the petitioner prefers Application / Representation before the department concerned respondent, the same shall be decided within 15 days’ time in accordance with the law and strictly on merits under intimation to this Court. In view of the above, this petition has served its’ purpose, therefore, it is hereby disposed of along with pending applications, if any.

Learned AAG assisted by the officer present in Court undertakes that in case the petitioner prefers Application / Representation before the department concerned respondent, the same shall be decided within 15 days’ time in accordance with the law and strictly on merits under intimation to this Court. In view of the above, this petition has served its’ purpose, therefore, it is hereby disposed of along with pending applications, if any. Judge Judge ARBROHI
